pattern: Counting distinct outcomes formed from a small multiset of digits is best done by checking availability of each candidate digit combination rather than enumerating physical arrangements.
hints: A 3-digit even number is fixed once you pick a hundreds digit, a tens digit, and an even units digit; the digits used must actually be available in the given multiset.

Track how many copies of each digit value you have, then try every hundreds/tens/units combination (hundreds nonzero, units even) and check the combination doesn't need more copies of a digit than you own.

Loop hundreds over 1-9, tens over 0-9, units over 0,2,4,6,8; for each triple, count how many times each digit value is needed and confirm your digit-count multiset covers it, counting the triple once if so.

edge_inputs.description: all same digit repeated exactly enough times
no even digit available so nothing can be formed
zero available as tens/units but not as leading hundreds
duplicated even digit used twice in one number
only two copies of a digit but a candidate needs three

stress.description: random small digit multisets across the allowed length range
